claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child I'm asking about.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that context. I'll help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for your child.

To get started, could you please tell me **the name of your child**?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now, to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to gather some key information.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 17.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful information.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good to know. Now I need to check a few more details about Alex's circumstances.

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has started an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's important information. Let me ask a follow-up question to clarify:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship a full-time apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's a full-time apprenticeship.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. I have one more important question:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?** (This relates to your residency and immigration status, which affects Child Benefit eligibility.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming you live in the UK. Just to clarify further for the eligibility assessment:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?** (This is about your immigration or residency status rather than just where you live.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Let me just confirm one final detail:

**Are you currently working or claiming any benefits, and if so, is your income above £60,000 per year?** (This is relevant because there's a High Income Child Benefit Tax Charge that may apply if your income exceeds this threshold, though you may still be eligible to claim.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into my income details right now - I just want to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's absolutely fine - I have enough information to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules and provide you with an outcome.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I need to inform you of an important issue regarding Alex's eligibility:

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you will **stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England**. 

Since Alex has started a full-time apprenticeship and is 17 years old, this means Alex would not be eligible for Child Benefit under the current rules, regardless of the fact that Alex is under 20 and living with you.

However, I want to clarify one important detail before I provide my final outcome:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship specifically in England, or is it in Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland?**

This is important because the rule about apprenticeships stopping Child Benefit eligibility specifically applies to apprenticeships in England.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we're in Manchester, so the apprenticeship is in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to provide you with a final decision.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

**You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you will stop receiving Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. This applies even though Alex is under 20 and living with you.

If you believe your circumstances may have changed or if you have further questions about this decision, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for additional guidance.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and living with you in Manchester. While Alex is under 20 and you are responsible for him/her, the eligibility rules state that you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child 'starts an apprenticeship in England'. Since Alex has started a full-time apprenticeship in England, Alex is no longer eligible for Child Benefit. This rule applies regardless of other factors such as living arrangements or your citizenship status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex because Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. The rules explicitly state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, and this takes priority over the general rule that allows Child Benefit to continue for children aged 16-19 in approved education or training.">
